meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Solving Debugging Woes: Antigravity Template for Students
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to debug faster, build professional SaaS, and level up your career.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about trial-and-error; it's about structural clarity. By deploying the Antigravity project template, you establish an automated, self-healing architecture. When paired with Claude Code, you transform your codebase into an interactive environment where logic flaws are identified, traced, and resolved through high-context AI analysis rather than manual guesswork.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of red stack traces, the clock is ticking, and the 'fix' you just applied broke three other features. This is the rite of passage every developer faces, but it shouldn't be your permanent state. The difference between a struggling student and a senior engineer is the ability to maintain architectural integrity while debugging.

The Antigravity project template serves as your scaffolding, providing the constraints and patterns necessary to keep your logic clean. When you combine this with the sheer analytical power of Claude Code, you stop fighting your syntax and start engineering solutions. It’s time to move beyond the frustration and build with absolute confidence.

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity
steps.description: Establish the foundation by cloning the boilerplate, which enforces strict directory structures that make debugging predictable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard boilerplate, ensuring strict TypeScript configuration and modular architecture.

steps.title: Contextualize the Bug
steps.description: Don't just paste an error. Feed the AI the surrounding logic and the specific state that triggered the failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current directory structure and the recent error logs to identify the root cause of the async state mismatch.

steps.title: Simulate Logic Paths
steps.description: Use the template's testing harness to isolate the failing function from the rest of your application stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an isolated test harness for the auth service to reproduce the race condition identified in the logs.

steps.title: Apply Self-Healing Patches
steps.description: Allow the AI to suggest structural refactors that fix the bug while adhering to the Antigravity design patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the state management flow to prevent the race condition, ensuring all changes align with the Antigravity middleware pattern.

steps.title: Verify and Validate
steps.description: Run the full test suite to ensure the fix hasn't introduced regressions in other modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the full regression suite and verify that the fix satisfies the initial requirements defined in the documentation.

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'debug-only' branch; never force patches into production without running a clean-room test.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation for your fixes; it turns your debugging sessions into a learning journal.
Leverage the Antigravity logging middleware to capture heap snapshots, which are invaluable for memory leak debugging.
When stuck, ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind the bug, not just the 'how' to fix it, to accelerate your learning.
Configure your IDE to watch the Antigravity config files, ensuring your project structure never drifts from the template.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is stable, focus on deploying your Antigravity build to a cloud provider and implementing automated CI/CD pipelines to catch bugs before they reach the browser.
content.conversion_block: You are closer than ever to shipping professional-grade software that actually works. Stop debugging blindly and start building with the systems that top-tier engineers use daily. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
